segments.text: Elihu continued:
Bear with me a little longer and I will show you that there is more to be said in God's behalf.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I get my knowledge from afar; I will ascribe justice to my Maker.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Be assured that my words are not false; one who has perfect knowledge is with you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: God is mighty, but despises no one; he is mighty, and firm in his purpose.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He does not keep the wicked alive but gives the afflicted their rights.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He does not take his eyes off the righteous; he enthrones them with kings and exalts them forever.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if people are bound in chains, held fast by cords of affliction,
He tells them what they have done— that they have sinned arrogantly.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He makes them listen to correction and commands them to repent of their evil.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If they obey and serve him, they will spend the rest of their days in prosperity and their years in contentment.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if they do not listen, they will perish by the sword and die without knowledge.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The godless in heart harbor resentment; even when he fetters them, they do not cry for help.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: They die in their youth, among male prostitutes of the shrines.
But those who suffer he delivers in their suffering; he speaks to them in their affliction.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He is wooing you from the jaws of distress to a spacious place free from restriction, to the comfort of your table laden with choice food.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But now you are laden with the judgment due the wicked; judgment and justice have taken hold of you.
Be careful that no one entices you by riches; do not let a large bribe turn you aside.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Would your wealth or even all your mighty efforts sustain you so you would not be in distress?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Do not long for the night, to drag people away from their homes.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Beware of turning to evil, which you seem to prefer to affliction.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: God is exalted in his power. Who is a teacher like him?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who has prescribed his ways for him, or said to him, 'You have done wrong'?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Remember to extol his work, which people have praised in song.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: All humanity has seen it; mortals gaze on it from afar.
How great is God—beyond our understanding! The number of his years is past finding out.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He draws up the drops of water, which distill as rain to the streams;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The clouds pour down their moisture and abundant showers fall on mankind.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who can understand how he spreads out the clouds, how he thunders from his pavilion?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: See how he scatters his lightning about him, bathing the depths of the sea.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: This is the way he governs the nations and provides food in abundance.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He fills his hands with lightning and commands it to strike its mark.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: His thunder announces the coming storm; even the cattle make known its approach.
segments.speaker: God
